title: Grafana 12.4 — Multi-property Variables
description: 12.4 added the ability to map many identifiers onto one logical variable value. This dashboard maps `dev`+`development` → `dev` and `prod`+`production` → `prod` so one `env` variable drives queries whether the label value is written long-form or short-form.

list.name: env
list.label: Environment
list.description: Grafana 12.4 multi-property variable — one option can carry multiple query values.

options.content:
### Multi-property variables (12.4)

The `env` variable has five display options:

| Display | Value sent to query |
|---|---|
| `dev`          | `dev`  |
| `development`  | `dev`  |
| `staging`      | `stg`  |
| `prod`         | `prod` |
| `production`   | `prod` |

Select *dev* and *development* — the query sees the SAME `dev` value in both cases and collapses to a single series. Before 12.4 you needed two variables or regex tricks to do this.

panels.title: up{env=~"${env:pipe}"} — regex-joined via the pipe formatter
